pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates file at this path with 555 Timer IC diagram containing components with wrong values (R1=100k, R2=1k) and 2 initial edges
setup_task.sh creates specification file detailing 555 Astable Multivibrator requirements with component list and wiring constraints
export_result.sh checks for PDF file at this target location after task completion
spec_file specifies 'R1: 1kΩ' as the correct resistor value (initial draft has 100k)
spec_file specifies 'R2: 10kΩ' as the correct resistor value (initial draft has 1k)
spec_file specifies 'C1: 10µF Electrolytic Capacitor' as required timing capacitor
spec_file specifies 'C2: 10nF Ceramic Capacitor' for noise filtering on Pin 5 (Control Voltage)
spec_file specifies 'R3: 470Ω (Output Current Limiter)' for LED protection
spec_file specifies 'D1: LED (Red)' connected to Pin 3 output through R3
verifier.py line 85 checks 'if total_edges >= 8' for full connectivity score (10 pts), contradicting metadata claim of 10

privileged_info_summary: The task is a synthetic 555 Timer Astable Multivibrator circuit correction exercise. The draft file is created at /home/ga/Diagrams/555_timer_draft.drawio with incorrect resistor values (R1=100k instead of 1k, R2=1k instead of 10k) and 2 initial edges. The specification file at /home/ga/Desktop/lab_circuit_spec.txt defines all required components: resistors 1kΩ and 10kΩ, capacitors 10µF and 10nF, output resistor 470Ω, and LED. All required string values in metadata are verified against the specification. The task expects a PDF export to /home/ga/Diagrams/exports/555_timer_final.pdf. A discrepancy exists: metadata claims min_edges of 10, but the verifier.py code only enforces a connectivity check at 8 or more edges for full points.
